```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  total <- sample(seq(80,160,10),1); b <- sample(30:70,1); ab <- sample(8:(b-5),1)
  ans <- ab/b
  vals <- round(c(ans, ab/total, b/total, (b-ab)/b,
                  (total-b)/total), 4)
  if(b<total && length(unique(vals))==5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.4f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Em `r total` registros, `r b` apresentam $B$ e `r ab` apresentam simultaneamente $A$ e $B$. Qual é $P(A\mid B)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
$$P(A\mid B)=\frac{P(A\cap B)}{P(B)}
=\frac{`r ab`}{`r b`}=`r sprintf("%.4f",ans)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S4Q1_probabilidade_condicional
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
